templates.template: Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active
templates.name: Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active
templates.description:
Comprehensive hardware monitoring for Raspberry Pi (all models).

Collects temperatures (all thermal zones), voltages, throttle status,
CPU/GPU frequencies, fan state, and NVMe temperatures via a single
master item script.

Setup:
1. Deploy script: /etc/zabbix/scripts/rpi-hw-stats.sh (chmod +x)
2. Deploy config: /etc/zabbix/zabbix_agent2.d/raspberry-pi.conf
3. Ensure zabbix user can run vcgencmd: sudo usermod -aG video zabbix
4. Restart agent: sudo systemctl restart zabbix-agent2

On Ubuntu, install vcgencmd: sudo apt install libraspberrypi-bin
On Raspberry Pi OS, vcgencmd is available by default.

Compatible with: Pi 2, Pi 3, Pi 4, Pi 5, Pi Zero 2 W

items.name: CPU max frequency
items.description: Maximum configured ARM CPU clock frequency.

items.name: Fan speed
items.description: Active cooler fan speed read from /sys/class/hwmon/*/fan1_input. Returns 0 when no tach-capable fan is connected. The DISCARD_VALUE error handler keeps the item supported when running an older script that does not yet emit fan_rpm.

items.name: Fan state
items.description: Pi 5 active cooler PWM state: 0=Off, 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Full, 255=No fan.

items.name: GPU frequency
items.description: Current VideoCore GPU clock frequency (vcgencmd measure_clock core).

items.name: Hardware stats (raw)
items.description: Master item: JSON blob with all hardware statistics from rpi-hw-stats.sh. Not stored — dependent items extract individual metrics.

triggers.expression: nodata(/Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active/rpi.hw.stats,{$RPI.NODATA})=1
triggers.name: No data received for {$RPI.NODATA}
triggers.description: The rpi-hw-stats.sh script has not returned data. Check that the Zabbix agent is running and the script is deployed correctly.

items.name: Power consumption
items.description: Total system power computed from PMIC rails (sum of V x A across all rails reported by vcgencmd pmic_read_adc). Pi 5 only — older Pis without PMIC monitoring report 0. The DISCARD_VALUE error handler keeps the item supported when running an older script that does not yet emit power_watts.

items.name: Throttle: ARM frequency capped
items.description: Bit 1 of get_throttled: ARM frequency is currently capped.

triggers.expression: last(/Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active/rpi.throttled.freq.capped)=1
triggers.name: ARM frequency capped
triggers.description: The CPU frequency is being limited, likely due to voltage or thermal constraints.

items.name: Throttle: frequency capping occurred since boot
items.description: Bit 17 of get_throttled: ARM frequency capping has occurred since boot.

items.name: Throttle status (raw)
items.description:
Raw throttle bitmask from vcgencmd get_throttled (hex).
Bits 0-3: current state. Bits 16-19: occurred since boot.

items.name: Throttle: soft temperature limit active
items.description: Bit 3 of get_throttled: soft temperature limit is currently active.

triggers.expression: last(/Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active/rpi.throttled.soft.temp.limit)=1
triggers.name: Soft temperature limit active
triggers.description: The soft temperature limit has been reached. The system is reducing performance to cool down.

items.name: Throttle: soft temp limit occurred since boot
items.description: Bit 19 of get_throttled: soft temperature limit has occurred since boot.

items.name: Throttle: human-readable summary
items.description: Decoded throttle bitmask: lists which conditions are active now and which have occurred since boot. "OK" when no throttle bits are set.

items.name: Throttle: currently throttled
items.description: Bit 2 of get_throttled: SoC is currently throttled.

triggers.expression: last(/Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active/rpi.throttled.throttled)=1
triggers.name: Currently throttled
triggers.description: The SoC is actively throttling due to thermal or power constraints. Performance is degraded.

items.name: Throttle: throttling occurred since boot
items.description: Bit 18 of get_throttled: throttling has occurred since boot.

items.name: Throttle: under-voltage detected
items.description:
Bit 0 of get_throttled: under-voltage detected right now.
Indicates the power supply is not delivering sufficient voltage.

triggers.expression: last(/Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active/rpi.throttled.undervoltage)=1
triggers.name: Under-voltage detected
triggers.description: The power supply is not delivering enough voltage. This causes instability, SD card corruption, and unexpected reboots. Replace the power supply or reduce load.

items.name: Throttle: under-voltage occurred since boot
items.description: Bit 16 of get_throttled: under-voltage has occurred at least once since boot. Sticky flag — stays 1 until reboot.

triggers.name: Under-voltage has occurred since boot
triggers.description: Under-voltage was detected at some point since boot. The power supply may be inadequate for the current load. Investigate even if the current state is normal.

discovery_rules.name: NVMe device discovery
discovery_rules.description: Discovers NVMe devices connected via M.2 HAT or USB adapter. Returns empty on Pis without NVMe storage.

item_prototypes.name: NVMe temperature: {#NVME.DEVICE}
item_prototypes.description: NVMe drive temperature for {#NVME.DEVICE} from hwmon sysfs.

trigger_prototypes.name: NVMe {#NVME.DEVICE}: Temperature > {$RPI.NVME.TEMP.DISASTER}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: NVMe drive {#NVME.DEVICE} has exceeded the disaster temperature threshold. Data loss risk.

trigger_prototypes.name: NVMe {#NVME.DEVICE}: Temperature > {$RPI.NVME.TEMP.HIGH}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: NVMe drive {#NVME.DEVICE} is running hot. Check airflow around the M.2 HAT.

item.host: Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active

discovery_rules.name: Thermal zone discovery
discovery_rules.description: Discovers all thermal zones from /sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone*. Pi 5 has cpu-thermal, gpu-thermal, rp1_adc-thermal. Pi 2/3/4 typically has cpu-thermal only.

item_prototypes.name: Temperature: {#ZONE.TYPE}
item_prototypes.description: Temperature for thermal zone {#ZONE} ({#ZONE.TYPE}) in degrees Celsius.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#ZONE.TYPE}: Temperature > {$RPI.TEMP.AVERAGE}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: Thermal zone {#ZONE.TYPE} has exceeded the average threshold. Performance may be affected soon.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#ZONE.TYPE}: Temperature > {$RPI.TEMP.DISASTER}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: Thermal zone {#ZONE.TYPE} has exceeded the disaster threshold. Hardware damage is possible. Shut down the device or improve cooling immediately.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#ZONE.TYPE}: Temperature > {$RPI.TEMP.HIGH}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: Thermal zone {#ZONE.TYPE} has exceeded the high threshold. Thermal throttling is imminent or active.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#ZONE.TYPE}: Temperature > {$RPI.TEMP.WARNING}°C
trigger_prototypes.description: Thermal zone {#ZONE.TYPE} is getting warm. Check ambient temperature and airflow.

item.host: Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active

discovery_rules.name: Voltage discovery
discovery_rules.description: Discovers voltage rails reported by vcgencmd: core, sdram_c, sdram_i, sdram_p. Returns empty if vcgencmd is unavailable.

item_prototypes.name: Voltage: {#VOLTAGE.TYPE}
item_prototypes.description: Voltage for rail {#VOLTAGE.TYPE} from vcgencmd measure_volts.

item.host: Raspberry Pi by Zabbix agent active

macros.description: No-data timeout for the master item.
SoC disaster temperature threshold (°C). Pi 5 throttles at 85°C.
